title: Third Encounter
text_warrior:
She comes in daylight this time.

This is new. The others are nearby — Saoirse is sharpening her blade forty meters back, Tierlan is somewhere in the tree line. They will not come. You do not know why you know this, but you do.

Kasyrra walks the road toward you with the unhurried certainty of someone arriving at a place they own. The mark responds to her presence the way a compass responds to north — not pointing, exactly, but orienting.

"Halfway," she says, when she is close enough not to need to raise her voice. She does not specify halfway to what. She does not need to. You feel the truth of it. "I came to see how you were carrying it."

She looks at you — actually looks, in a way that takes inventory. Whatever she is counting, she seems satisfied.

"You're angry," she says. Not an accusation. An observation. "Good. The ones who stop being angry stop noticing. I'd rather have your attention."

She reaches out, not to the mark this time, but to your jaw — a brief touch, the back of two fingers, barely contact at all. It is not warm. It is something older than warm.

"The next part will be harder," she says. "Not painful. Just... more than you've been prepared for." A pause. "You've been prepared for more than most."

She turns and walks back the way she came, and when Saoirse rounds the bend two minutes later, the road is empty.
text_scholar:
She comes in daylight this time.

This is new. The others are nearby — Mireille is forty meters back reviewing her notes, Tierlan somewhere ahead reading the ground. They will not come. You do not know why you know this, but you do.

Kasyrra walks the road toward you with the unhurried certainty of someone arriving at a place they own. The mark responds to her presence in a way you have been trying to document for two weeks now, and still lack the vocabulary for.

"Halfway," she says, when she is close enough not to need to raise her voice. She does not specify halfway to what. She does not need to. You feel the truth of it in the data you've been accumulating. "I came to see how the work was holding."

She looks at you — takes inventory. You try to do the same to her, and find that she permits it. This is itself interesting.

"You've been recording the changes accurately," she says. "Most people edit their own observations. You don't. That's rare." A pause. "Useful."

She reaches out, not to the mark, but to your jaw — a brief touch that your instruments have no category for. It is not warm. It is something older than warm.

"The next phase will produce data you won't want to record," she says. "Record it anyway. That's when the methodology matters most."

She turns and walks back the way she came. You write for a long time before you can describe what you observed. Some of it you write twice.
text_scout:
She comes in daylight this time.

You felt her approach a full minute before she appeared — the pressure shift, the organized silence spreading ahead of her through the trees. You turned and waited, because that is what the data said to do.

Kasyrra walks the road toward you. The others are nearby. They will not come. You've noticed the pattern now: whenever she arrives, the world around her narrows to just you. You have been trying to determine if this is a spell or simply her. You have not yet concluded.

"Halfway," she says, when she is close enough. She does not specify halfway to what. You already know. You've been charting the change in the spaces between sleep, reading the evidence your own body is leaving. "I came to see how you were moving with it."

She looks at you — reads you, the way you read terrain. You let her, because the information exchange goes both ways and you want to know what she's finding.

"You stopped fighting the adaptation," she says. Not quite approval. "Smarter than most. Fighting it costs more than it delays."

She reaches out and touches your jaw, briefly — the back of two fingers. Whatever it is, it is not warmth. It is something older.

"The next part will ask more of your instincts than your training," she says. "Trust the instincts. Your training was built for a different territory."

She turns and goes. The forest's silence follows her at the same distance it always does, and then it stops.
text_default:
She comes in daylight this time.

The others are nearby. They will not come. You do not know why you know this, but you do.

Kasyrra walks the road toward you with the certainty of someone arriving at a place they own. The mark responds to her presence the way a compass responds to north.

"Halfway," she says, when she is close enough. She does not specify halfway to what. You feel the truth of it.

She looks at you — takes inventory. Whatever she is counting, she seems satisfied.

"You've been carrying it well," she says. A pause. "The next part will be harder. Not painful. Just more than you've been prepared for."

She reaches out and touches your jaw, briefly — the back of two fingers. Not warmth. Something older.

She turns and goes. The road is empty when the others arrive.

companion_reactions.saoirse: Saoirse arrives thirty seconds after Kasyrra's gone. She reads the road where Kasyrra stood, then looks at you. 'I could feel it,' she says. That's all she says.
companion_reactions.mireille: Mireille writes 'Daylight. Observable duration approx. 4 minutes. She wanted to be seen coming. Why?' and stares at the page for a long time.
companion_reactions.tierlan: Tierlan comes from the tree line at an angle that would have given him a sight line. He says nothing about what he may or may not have seen. He walks the rest of the day slightly closer to you than usual.

aftermath: Something has shifted in the quality of the mark's presence — not stronger, exactly. More awake. As if it has received an instruction you were not meant to hear.
